result:
Done. Shipped to main (commit `0bdaf3a`).

## What changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `app/api/db/migrate/route.ts` | Reorder maps + insert Map 11; shift heap orders |
| `app/api/db/seed-map11/route.ts` | New — 25 heaps (orders 26–50), 125 essential words |
| `components/MapView.tsx` | Added `sky` accent for the `essential` theme |

**Map 11 "Essential Bulgarian"** displays as the **2nd tab** (after Beginners Bay) via `order_index=2`. Maps 2–10 slid down to slots 3–11, and their heaps shifted from orders 26–250 → 51–275, freeing 26–50 for Map 11.

**The 25 heaps** (5 words each, simple practical names): Basic/More Pronouns, People, Family, Relatives, Yes & No, Joining Words, Position Words, Directions, Time Words, Days & Weeks, three Verb heaps + Doing Things, Question Words, Personal Info, Food, Fruit & Veg, The Home, Around Town, Open & Closed, Money & Life, Size & Speed, Describing Things. All the MUST-include pronouns and core verbs are in. Uses `insertHeapsDeduped` so no word collides with existing heaps.

## One thing I did differently
The task's literal SQL (`order_index + 1`, `"order" + 25`) is **not idempotent** — but the migrate endpoint is designed to be re-run safely (everything else uses `ON CONFLICT`/`IF NOT EXISTS`). A second migrate run with raw increments would double-shift and corrupt the DB, which git can't revert. So I **guarded both shifts** with sentinels (map 2 still at `order_index=2` / map 2's first heap still at order 26) so re-running migrate is a clean no-op. Same end result, safe to re-run.

**Note:** Five family words from your list (`майка/баща/брат/сестра` + `син`/son, which collides with `син`/blue) already exist in Map 1, so I used `съпруг, съпруга, баба, дядо, внук, леля, чичо, братовчед` instead to keep the family heaps full and avoid silent dedup drops.

Build passes. The 24 lint errors are all pre-existing (PWAProvider etc.) — none in the files I touched. Did not run any seed/migrate endpoints against the DB (code-only, as instructed).
